characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Animal-Motif Team: Les Pyjamasques, Tatouro and Les Mascrapules, have suits and names that are based on various animals. Calling Your Attacks: Some of the heroes pretty much always call their super powers by name when using them, usually by saying their superhero names or animal route at the beginning sentence of their power. The Chosen One: The totem cat, owl and lizard ended up choosing Sacha, Amaya and Greg (albeit by accident) after falling to Earth to help save the rest of the totem animals from Sorceline's clutches. Fusion Dance: The trio turn into their superhero forms by becoming one with their respective totem animals. They can transform back into their civilian forms by releasing the animals. Henshin Hero: The Pyjamasques actively transform from regular, non-superpowered kids into their superpowered alter-egos by their magical friends, the totem animals.

characters.name: Sacha/Yoyo
characters.description: Animal Motifs: In the original books, his motif was that of a tiger, but became a normal cat in later volumes of the series. Blue Is Heroic: Wears blue in both his civilian and superhero forms. Color-Coded Characters: Blue. Fast as Lightning: In later volumes of the series, he leaves lightning in his trail when using his super speed.

characters.name: Amaya/Bibou
characters.description: Badass Cape: In her costume, she wears a red cape with feathers. Blow You Away: Being an owl-themed hero gives one the advantage of using a lot of wind-based attacks. Lady Looks Like a Dude: Given the fact she had rounder eyes and lacked eyelashes in the first 18 books, Bibou was long thought to be male like her teammates. Token Flyer: The only one out of the three heroes that can fly.

characters.name: Greg/Gluglu
characters.description: Animal Motifs: Lizards. Chameleon Camouflage: Fitting with his reptilian powers' theme, Gluglu can gaininvisibility. Early Adaptation Weirdness: In one of Les Pyjamasques original stories, Gluglu's costume initially had the ability to breathe underwater. Odd Name Out: When it comes to the French names, he's the only one of the trio whose civilian and hero names start with the same letter.

characters.name: Tatouro
characters.description: Adaptational Intelligence: Unlike Armadylan, Tatouro is often portrayed as adimwittedhero. Animal Motifs: Armadillo. Anti-Hero: Due to being dimwitted and seeing the heroes and villains fighting each other, he often thinks that he is supposed to be the villain. The Big Guy: He's noticeably taller than the other heroes, and even stronger than Gluglu. Dumb Muscle: He's very strong, but not particularly bright.

characters.name: Lilifée
characters.description: An Ice Person: She has the power over ice. Puppy Love: Lilifée and Les Pyjamasques seem to show this. But in the early volumes of the book, this was generally downplayed a bit due to the primary protagonists fighting for Lilifée's affections.

characters.name: Energuman
characters.description: Expy: He is meant to be a parody ofthe Dark Knighthimself.

characters.name: Totem Animals
characters.description: A Boy and His X: Totem animals form life-long bonds with the children they have chosen to watch over. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: Among the animals seen are a pink rhino and a blue cat. The Chooser of the One: They decide which child they will watch over and become their protector for the rest of that's child's life. Distressed Dude: In "La Legende de Pyjamasques", Sorceline imprisons the totem animals to prevent kids from being able to dream like her. Fortunately, they are saved when the owl, cat and lizard totems manage to escape and meet the three kids who would become Les Pyjamasques. Dream Walker: They are able to enter childrens' dreams.

characters.name: Roméo Mécano
characters.description: A.I. Is a Crapshoot: At one point, Romeo's Kisses Machine in "Les Pyjamasques et la machine à bisous" turns against his creatorand gives him a spanking for harassing the Pyjamasques.. Big Bad: Averted. Unlike his television counterpart, he tends to make recurring appearances in the book series. Brainy Brunette: Has blue hair and relies on inventions of his own creation to make trouble. Butt-Monkey: As of "Les Pyjamasques et operation zero", he often gets mocked and taunted by the heroes and other characters for the spiky shape of his hair and most of his misfortunes. Child Prodigy: Is around the same age as the PJ Masks, and has built some seriously advanced machines.

characters.name: Sorceline, the Mitomites, and the Papinuits
characters.description: Color-Coded Characters: Sorceline dresses entirely in black. Evil Wears Black: As stated above, Sorceline's outfit of choice is entirely black. Hover Board: Sorceline's Aero-Lune. The Insomniac: She is unable to sleep or dream, which is why she tries to capture the animal totems for herself. Lesser of Two Evils: Averted. Sorceline is seen to be more dangerous and homicidal than Luna Girl in the book series such as in "La Légende des Pyjamasques" where she tries to strike the Pyjamasques with her lightning-powered magnet and trap them in a magical bag that makes anything and anyone disappear into oblivion.

characters.name: Ninjaka, the Ninjazouaves
characters.description: Adorable Evil Minions: The Ninjazouaves, with how they're smaller than our heroes and villains, speak gibberish and pretty accident-prone. Ambiguously Human: The Ninjazouaves have a humanoid appearance, and some of them are revealed to have names, but there is still the fact that they talk in their own gibberish, are smaller than even the other kid characters, and we never see what they look like underneath their masks. Color-Coded Characters: Teal for Ninjaka, purple for the Ninjzouaves. Gratuitous Ninja: Is this to the books, which is set in modern times but features a ninja anyway. Highly Visible Ninja: Averted. Unlike his television counterpart (Night Ninja), Ninjaka tends to focus on being slightly stealthy via his motives.

characters.name: Les Mascrapules
characters.description: Adapted Out: Unlike Roméo, Sorceline and Ninkjaka, they have yet to appear in the TV series. Animal Motifs: Their powers are based on a rat (Grigri), a fly (Zina), and a worm (Ulbert). Evil Counterpart: of Les Pyjamasques. Sizeshifter: Grigri can shrink down to the size of a mouse.

characters.name: Connor/Catboy
characters.description: Ambiguously Brown: Has a darker complexion when compared to Amaya and Greg in the cartoon, especially noticeable during the daytime scenes. This suggests he might be Latino, but we never see any of the characters' parents so it's difficult to determine any kind of origin for him. However, Catboy's skintone is exactly the same as Gekko and Owlette's in the merchandise. Blue Is Heroic: Leader of the team and wears blue in both his civilian and superhero forms. Brainy Brunette: Is noted for being smart. Cats Hate Water: Is terrified of water, as both Connor and Catboy. Luna Girl exploits this in "Catboy's Cloudy Crisis". Color-Coded Characters: Blue.

characters.name: Amaya/Owlette
characters.description: Ambiguously Bi: Besides her aforementionedCoy, Girlish Flirt PoseandFlirty Voice Ploymoments with Catboy and Howler, there's also her interactions with An Yu during season 6'sAn Yu's Birthday. Badass Bookworm: Before heading off to stop a Nighttime Villain she spends her time reading a book. Badass Cape: In her costume, she wears a red cape with feathers. Beautiful Singing Voice: Owlette has this in "Storm of the Ninja" and "Moon Music." Beauty Equals Goodness: Besides being thesmart oneof the team, she's a very pretty girl, both as Amaya and as Owlette, which is enough for Pirate Robot to go gaga over her.

characters.name: Greg/Gekko
characters.description: Animal Motifs: Lizards. Attack of the 50-Foot Whatever: In "Super-Sized Gekko", he got big thanks to the super-sizer in this episode. The Baby of the Bunch: All three of them are the same age, but he's the youngest. Badly Battered Babysitter: "Terrible Two-Some" turns him into this when he has to look after Catboy and Owlette after Romeo's Baby-Beam regresses their minds to those of toddlers. Chameleon Camouflage: Fitting with his reptilian powers' theme, Gekko can gaininvisibility.

characters.name: PJ Robot
characters.description: A.I. Is a Crapshoot: A rare heroic case, PJ Robot grew so fond of the PJ Masks that he eventually turned against Romeo and joined the heroes. Becoming the Mask: As stated above, he was originally created by Romeo to spy on the heroes, but ended up becoming friends with them instead. Beware the Nice Ones: When motivated by the power of friendship to defend the PJ Masks against Night Ninja, he extends histool armsto be more intimidating. Night Ninja takes one look at the power tools and decidesanywhere elsewould be a good place to be rather than standing in front of PJ Robot. Gadgeteer Genius: He is responsible for creating and modifying several of the PJ Masks' tools and vehicles, including their space suits and the PJ Seeker. Good Costume Switch: Gekko gives him a new paint job when the PJ's welcome him on their team.

characters.name: Dylan/Armadylan
characters.description: Adaptational Intelligence: Unlike Tatouro, who wasThe Ditz, Armadylan knows the difference between being a hero and a villain and hasHidden Depths, but is more gullible and hot-headed. Advertised Extra: In Season 6, Armadylan received aPromotion to Opening Titlesand was prominently featured on several advertisement posters, but ultimately appeared in only two episodes during the second half of the season, and never officially joined the Power Heroes team. Animal-Themed Superbeing: An armadillo. Anti-Hero: He wants to be a hero, but is not cut out to be one and often causes more problems than he solves. Bash Brothers: With Gekko, especially whenever an episode calls for them to team up to take down a villain.

characters.name: The Dragon/An Yu
characters.description: Apologetic Attacker: She apologizes to Owlette near the end of part 1 of "Meet An Yu", telling her that she must obey Night Ninja once more, before blowing her away with her breath. Ascended Extra: She started off as a recurring character following her debut in season 3, but becomes a full-fledged member of the team in season 6. Brought Down to Normal: Until the Season 5 finale "Luna's Moon Attack," her powers only worked on Mystery Mountain. Hence, she lost them any time she left the mountain. This is no longer an issue in Season 6; from the season's premiere "Heroes Everywhere" onwards, she is now able to use them outside of the mountain. Defrosting Ice Queen: She was rather antisocial in her first few appearances, but gradually learned to loosen up and be more willing to accept help from the PJ's. Early-Bird Cameo: Her dragon form first appears in the Season 2 episode "The Dragon Gong", but she doesn’t appear in her human form until season 3’s "Meet An Yu".

characters.name: Newton/Newton Star
characters.description: Adorably Precocious Child: He's one smart cookie and one smart superhero to boot. Aesop Amnesia: A recurring one with him is learning to not to dwell on his mistakes whenever he messes something up. Ascended Extra: Originally a recurring character in seasons 4 & 5, he eventually becomes a full member of the team in season 6. Batman Can Breathe in Space: He doesn't need a space suit or helmet when going into space. Black and Nerdy: His favorite activity in his spare time is hanging out in the library, studying anything space related. And he wearsNerd Glassesduring the day.

characters.name: Teeny Weeny Ninjalino/The Splat Monster
characters.description: Ambiguously Human: Teeny Weeny has a humanoid appearance, but there is still the fact that he talks in his own gibberish and is unusually small at just a couple of inches. We also never see what he looks like underneath his mask. Heel–Face Turn: As of "Teeny Weeny Returns", he turned against Night Ninja and decided to work at the heroes' side. As of "Teeny Weeny to the Rescue", he is an official defender of Mystery Mountain along with An Yu. Homing Projectile: One thing that makes Teeny Weeny so dangerous as the Splat Monster, is that he can telepathically control the sticky splat projectiles he fires, making dodging impossible. Hulking Out: He transforms into much bigger, stronger form when he sneezes. Lilliputians: He is barely a few inches tall.

characters.name: The PJ Pets
characters.description: Action Pet: Lionel is Gekko's pet, and becomes his sidekick once Gekko gets his powers back. To a lesser degree, Owly and Alley Cat, though they are technically not Owlette and Catboy's pets and more likely a wild owl and stray cat respectively. All Animals Are Dogs: Lionel behaves much more like a domesticated dog than a lizard. Among other things, he likes to play fetch. Amazing Technicolor Population: Alley Cat initially had normal grey fur, but has blue fur from season 5 onwards. Cats Are Mean: Averted; Alley Cat is as heroic as Catboy is. Cute Owl: Owly certainly qualifies.

characters.name: PJ Riders
characters.description: 24-Hour Armor: They’re always shown wearing their saddles/riding armor. Adaptational Intelligence: The original Totem Animals were capable of human speech, whereas the PJ Riders are not but can at least understand it. Adaptational Skill: The Riders possess the same powers as the PJ Masks, but none of the abilities of their book counterparts. Adaptation Name Change: Their book counterparts were named Yahto, Oolgee and Chu'a; here they are Cat Stripe King, Eagle Owl and Power Lizard. Adaptation Species Change: Downplayed example with Cat Stripe King; his book counterpart Yahto was a full-blooded tiger, but in the television series he seems to be a cross between both a tiger and a cheetah.

characters.name: Ivan/Ice Cub
characters.description: Animal-Themed Superbeing: A polar bear. Ascended Fanboy: He is a huge fan of superheroes, and later becomes one himself. Bear Hug: One of his powers is being able to melt frozen objects by hugging them. He also gives an actual bear hug to Catboy at the end of "I Scream for Wolfies". Beary Friendly: Gets turned into a polar bear superhero from the astroid fragment, and still keeps his friendly attitude. Beware the Nice Ones: He's easily one of the kindest and most pleasant characters, which can make you forget that he's still a superhero whose motif is that of apolar bear.

characters.name: Lilyfay
characters.description: Achilles' Heel: Grows weaker when she's not under the stars for a period of time. Adaptational Badass: She’s much more willing to fight than her book counterpart, who was more passive and avoided confrontation. Adaptational Hairstyle Change: Downplayed; she still has blond hair in pigtails, but her hair is straight rather than curly. Adaptational Modesty: She wears a jumpsuit under her dress in the TV series, whereas her book counterpart does not. Batman Can Breathe in Space: Like Newton, she can freely travel through space without protective gear.

characters.name: Bastet
characters.description: Animal-Themed Superbeing: A serval. Arch-Enemy: To Pharaoh Boy, even though they likely fought before "It's a Cat Thing." Dislikes the New Guy: After Armadylan joins the group in "The Curse of Armadylan", she spends most of the episode acting rather hostile towards him due to mistaking him for an ancient armadillo villain, even attacking him with her Sun Disc. She eventually warms up to him when he helps her defeat Pharaoh Boy. Furry Reminder: She behaves like an actual cat even moreso than Catboy, including walking on all fours, nuzzling up against PJ Robot, making feline vocalizations, and even grooming herself like a cat. Good All Along: In her debut episode, she was mistakenly believed to be Pharaoh Boy’s minion, before the misunderstanding was cleared up.

characters.name: Luna Girl, Motsuki and the moths
characters.description: Alpha Bitch: Her personality leans toward this. Annoying Younger Sibling: Motsuki acts as one to Luna Girl after becoming humanoid and being adopted by Luna as her little sister. Exaggerated in "Luna Goes Too Far," when the both of them decided to go their separate ways, even though they're now more like enemies than sisters, which lasted all the way up until "Luna's Moon Attack." Bad Boss: Luna Girl frequently insults her moths and often tries to replace them even after she promised she wouldn't in one episode. In "Ninja Moths" they actually attempt to kill her and in "Catboy and the Butterfly Brigade" they try to leave her. Batman Can Breathe in Space: Anytime the characters visit the moon, Luna Girl has no trouble breathing while there. Unlike the PJ Masks, who need to wear helmets, and (in "Moon Breaker") Romeo, who had to stay inside his lab. The season 3 episode "Best Friends Forever" reveals that it's the luna magnet that grants her this ability; when the magnet began to run out of power while Luna Girl was out in space, she had to hold her breath. Motsuki also has this power. Beast Woman: Motsuki, after her second transformation in "Moth on the Moon."

characters.name: Night Ninja and the Ninjalinos
characters.description: Adorable Evil Minions: The Ninjalinos, with how they're smaller than our heroes and villains, speak gibberish and pretty accident-prone. Alliterative Name:NightNinja. Ambiguous Gender: Aside from Daisy, it is unknown if there are any other female Ninjalinos, given the lack of sexual characteristics. Ambiguously Human: The Ninjalinos have a humanoid appearance, and three of them have names (Marvin, Daisy, and Teeny-Weeny), but there is still the fact that they talk in their own gibberish, are smaller than even the other kid characters (with Teeny Weeny being unnaturally small at only a couple of inches), and we never see what they look like underneath their masks. Color-Coded Characters: Teal for Night Ninja, purple for the Ninjalinos.

characters.name: Romeo and his robots
characters.description: A.I. Is a Crapshoot: While his robots are usually loyal, they can turn on him on occasion. The two most famous examples being PJ Robot, who joined the heroes, and Pirate Robot, who was originally created to be a chef. Bad Boss: Not nearly to the extent of Luna Girl and Night Ninja, but he sometimes talks down to his robots, andblames them for his defeats. Benevolent Boss: Sometimes toward Robot such as in "Nobody's Sidekick", "PJ Masks Save Christmas" and "Christmas Time with PJ Masks". Big Bad: The most frequent villain to appear in the series and was the first one to appear, too. Bizarre Taste in Food: His favorite ice cream flavor is apparently double-tofu pistachio.

characters.name: The Wolfy Kids
characters.description: Bullying a Dragon: The episode"The Prank Wheelz"has them come across Armadylan at a restaurant, when Howler decides to use a drink cup and a rubber duck to play a prank on him. Needless to say Armadylan's not very happy at being pranked and uses"Rolling Thunder"to chase after them. Even Gekko (who was watching their prank on video at HQ) says that pranking him was a bad idea. Catchphrase: "Yip-Yip-Yaroo!", and "Wolfin' It Up!". "Shackadoodle" also pops up occasionally. Childish Tooth Gap: Howler has a visible one. Cool Car: Their Wolf Wheelz, which may possibly be sentient. The Ditz: Kevin is the dumbest of the entire cast by a long shot.

characters.name: Munki-Gu
characters.description: Animal Supervillain: He's a monkey, and one of the nighttime villains. Banana Peel: One of his favorite pranks. Fitting, since bananas are hisTrademark Favorite Food. Mischief-Making Monkey: He's a monkey supervillain that only lives for causing mischief. Put on a Bus: He is one of two villains absent from Season 6, the other one being Orticia. Sealed Evil in a Can: He was trapped in the form of a statue for 1000 years, but is set free again due to Night Ninja's actions in "PJ Party Mountain" and Gekko accidentally patting the statue on the head three times.

characters.name: Octobella and Percival
characters.description: Arch-Enemy: To Gekko, making her the only Nighttime villain so far who serves this role to a single PJ Mask rather than the whole team. Bad Boss: Though not as bad as Luna Girl, in the episode "Magnet in the Moat," she is seen regularly antagonizing Percival, much to his annoyance. Beauty Is Bad: She's one of the nastier villains in the show, and at the same time, she just so happens to be quite a beautiful-looking octopus-girl. Berserk Button: Whatever you do, never, EVER, steal her pondweed. Dreadful Musician: She doesn't have a good singing voice. Yet Armadylan complemented it when he first heard it.

characters.name: Pharaoh Boy
characters.description: Adaptational Attractiveness: He is loosely based on Apophis, the antagonist from the "Les Pyjamasques et la momie d’Apophis" two-parter. But unlike Apophis, who looked more like a typical mummy, Pharaoh Boy has no bandages covering his face, which in turn shows no signs of decay. Despite this, he is still named Apophis in the French dub. Adaptational Wimp: While he certainly has several magic abilities, he lacks some of his book counterpart's more impressive powers. Apophis was basically a god with the power toturn into a giant, mummified snake, and use his bandages as a weapon. Another Dimension: When he's not in the city causing trouble, Pharaoh Boy resides in the World Beyond, which he can enter and leave through a portal in a sphinx statue. Fish out of Temporal Water: Much like An Yu, the fact that he hails from ancient times makes him unfamiliar with the modern world. Unlike her however,he doesn't let this stop him from going out into the city. Grand Theft Me: He can possess animals (or at least, cats), and can also forcefully inflict this on others.

characters.name: Orticia
characters.description: Adaptational Backstory Change: Downplayed. Like in the book, she was created by Romeo from a seed and some chemicals. However, in the book Night Ninja (called Ninjaka in France) also played a part in her creation; he tried to steal Romeo's invention to grow a bamboo forest, and in their struggle the villains ended up spilling the chemicals, creating Orticia. Adaptational Villainy: Her book counterpart was more a case ofNot Evil, Just Misunderstood, who only fought Les Pyjamasques because they attacked her, and she eventually took their advice to use her powers to make the city prettier. Here, she is full-on a villain who didn't care how much trouble her plants caused and was initially unwilling to listen to the PJ's. Dreadful Musician: Averted; the episode "Orticia and the Pumpkins" shows that she is actuallygreatat singing compared to Octobella. Eco-Terrorist: A G-rated version. She (initially) only cares about growing her plants wherever she wants, regardless of who or what gets in her way. Expy: She's essentially a G-rated version of Poison Ivy.

characters.name: Pirate Robot, Hornswoggle & Sally
characters.description: A.I. Is a Crapshoot:Pirate Robot was supposed to be a chef for Romeo, but after becoming a pirate instead, he immediately ditched his creator to become a villain in his own right.Sally used to be a normal Fly Bot namedFlybot337B, until, inspired by Pirate Robot, she rebelled against Romeo and joined Pirate Robot. Pirate Robot was supposed to be a chef for Romeo, but after becoming a pirate instead, he immediately ditched his creator to become a villain in his own right. Sally used to be a normal Fly Bot namedFlybot337B, until, inspired by Pirate Robot, she rebelled against Romeo and joined Pirate Robot. Cool Boat: In his second episode, he builds himself a flying pirate ship named the Spanner of the Skies. Distracted by the Sexy: Pirate Robot admires Owlette and calls her "Pirate Queen." (Not to be confused with Risky Boots, the self-titled "queen of the seven seas.")

characters.name: The Speedy Twins - Carly and Cartoka
characters.description: Aerith and Bob: Carly is a common name in real life, while Cartoka is not. Badass Driver: Carly is the driver of the duo, who pilots the Flashcar. And she is good at it.Cartoka also fits into this, as he pilots the Gigantogarage. Cartoka also fits into this, as he pilots the Gigantogarage. The Bad Guy Wins: In their debut episode, they managed to steal and wreckallof the PJ Masks vehicles, with the heroes ultimately being unable to retrieve them and gaining the PJ Riders as a result despite the fact Catboy said that they'll get their vehicles back soon.However, the PJ Masks finally retrieved and repaired theirThememobilesin "The PJ Riders Save the Day,"complete with new powers similar to the ones the PJ's gained in "PJ Power Up,". However, the PJ Masks finally retrieved and repaired theirThememobilesin "The PJ Riders Save the Day,"complete with new powers similar to the ones the PJ's gained in "PJ Power Up,".

characters.name: Gloop the Third and the Gloopets
characters.description: Aliens Are Bastards: He loves covering everything in Gloop, and that is the sole reason he came to Earth. Aliens Speaking English: Averted for the Gloopets, who speak in their own language. Gloop the Third does speak English, but this is clearly stated to be thanks to a translator built into his suit. Heel–Face Turn: Being double-crossed by Romeo and nearly having his home planet destroyed leads to him allying himself with the Power Heroes. Orcus on His Throne: While Gloop does personally oversee his plans, he mostly lets the Gloopets do the fighting rather than getting his own hands dirty. Pet the Dog: Despite his annoyance at the Gloopets screwing things up, the season six finale shows that he really does care about them.

characters.name: Cameron
characters.description: Ambiguously Brown: When compared to his sister, Marie. Said sister has been seen holding the hand of a black woman whom we can assume is their mother, implying they are either of mixed race or adopted. Color-Coded Characters: Orange. Demoted to Extra: As of Season 4 and onwards, Cameron is reduced from being a minor character to a background character with occasional appearances. Jerkass Has a Point: Obnoxious he can be at times, he did have a right to be angry at Greg for losing his toy car since he entrusted him with taking care of it. He does later apologize when Greg brings it back (after Luna Girl had stolen it) and owns up to it. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: He's rough around the edges and occasionally teases the heroes, but he's on good terms with them and his classmates.

characters.name: Teacher
characters.description: Cool Teacher: The PJ Masks certainly consider him one, judging by their reactions in "Teacher Goes Ninja". That episode he qualified, though in an evil way, by temporarily becoming a skilled ninja. No Name Given: Is simply known as "Teacher." Real Men Wear Pink: His pyjamas are pink, with a strawberry pattern.

characters.name: Jayden Housten
characters.description: The Ghost: Hasn't made a physical appearance in the show and is only recognized through posters. Vague Age: Could easily be aTeen Idolbut might be in his early twenties.

characters.name: Master Fang
characters.description: Heroes Prefer Swords: His weapon of choice is a sword. One episode focussed on Night Ninja stealing it from the tv-show, and the PJ Masks had to get it back. Old Master: He's an old martial artist.

characters.name: KickMcGee
characters.description: The Ghost: Frequently mentioned along with Master Fang, but so far he has never been seen. Sidekick: To Master Fang.

characters.name: Flossy Flash
characters.description: Action Girl: A female hero who, if Amaya is to be believed, can kick ass. Girl-Show Ghetto:In-Universeexample that is played straight in season 1, where Amaya is the only character who is a fan of her. Averted as of Season 2, when Armadylan is revealed to be a fan as well. Purple Is Powerful: A powerful superheroine in an all purple costume. Telepathy: She can tell where people are simply by thinking.
